-lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,320p' harness/task_queue.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"SQLite-backed task and stage queue.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport uuid\\\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable, Optional\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.db import HarnessDB\\\\nfrom harness.models import (\\\\n    PermissionRequestRecord,\\\\n    PlannedStage,\\\\n    QuotaEventRecord,\\\\n    RiskLevel,\\\\n    StageRecord,\\\\n    StageStatus,\\\\n    TaskRecord,\\\\n    TaskStatus,\\\\n)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef utcnow_iso() -> str:\\\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass TaskQueue:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"CRUD layer for tasks, stages, and audit tables.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, db: HarnessDB) -> None:\\\\n        self.db = db\\\\n\\\\n    def create_task(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        title: str,\\\\n        description: str,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        goal: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        complexity: str = \\\\\\\"medium\\\\\\\",\\\\n        priority: int = 50,\\\\n        status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING,\\\\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        task_id: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        record = TaskRecord(\\\\n            task_id=task_id or uuid.uuid4().hex,\\\\n            title=title,\\\\n            description=description,\\\\n            goal=goal,\\\\n            verify_cmd=verify_cmd,\\\\n            complexity=complexity,\\\\n            priority=priority,\\\\n            status=status,\\\\n            working_dir=working_dir,\\\\n            created_at=now,\\\\n            updated_at=now,\\\\n        )\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                INSERT INTO tasks (\\\\n                    task_id, title, description, goal, verify_cmd, complexity,\\\\n                    priority, status, working_dir, created_at, updated_at\\\\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    record.task_id,\\\\n                    record.title,\\\\n                    record.description,\\\\n                    record.goal,\\\\n                    record.verify_cmd,\\\\n                    record.complexity,\\\\n                    record.priority,\\\\n                    record.status.value,\\\\n                    record.working_dir,\\\\n                    record.created_at,\\\\n                    record.updated_at,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n        return record\\\\n\\\\n    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def list_tasks(self, status: Optional[TaskStatus] = None) -> list[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\\\\n        if status is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\" WHERE status = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params = (status.value,)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC\\\\\\\"\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_task(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def pick_next_runnable_task(self) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM tasks\\\\n                WHERE status IN (?, ?, ?)\\\\n                ORDER BY\\\\n                    CASE status\\\\n                        WHEN ? THEN 0\\\\n                        WHEN ? THEN 1\\\\n                        ELSE 2\\\\n                    END,\\\\n                    priority DESC,\\\\n                    created_at ASC\\\\n                LIMIT 1\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PLANNING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            ).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (status.value, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def update_task_working_dir(self, task_id: str, working_dir: str) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET working_dir = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (working_dir, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def save_stages(self, task_id: str, stages: Iterable[PlannedStage]) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        records: list[StageRecord] = []\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            for planned in stages:\\\\n                record = StageRecord(\\\\n                    stage_id=uuid.uuid4().hex,\\\\n                    task_id=task_id,\\\\n                    stage_type=planned.stage_type,\\\\n                    stage_order=planned.stage_order,\\\\n                    model_role=planned.model_role,\\\\n                    assigned_model=None,\\\\n                    assigned_provider=None,\\\\n                    status=StageStatus.PENDING,\\\\n                    handoff_doc_path=None,\\\\n                    result_summary=None,\\\\n                    token_used=0,\\\\n                    duration_sec=0.0,\\\\n                    started_at=None,\\\\n                    finished_at=None,\\\\n                    retry_count=0,\\\\n                    verify_cmd=planned.verify_cmd,\\\\n                    metadata=dict(planned.metadata),\\\\n                )\\\\n                conn.execute(\\\\n                    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                    INSERT INTO stages (\\\\n                        stage_id, task_id, stage_type, stage_order, model_role,\\\\n                        assigned_model, assigned_provider, status, handoff_doc_path,\\\\n                        result_summary, token_used, duration_sec, started_at,\\\\n                        finished_at, retry_count, verify_cmd, metadata\\\\n                    ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    (\\\\n                        record.stage_id,\\\\n                        record.task_id,\\\\n                        record.stage_type,\\\\n                        record.stage_order,\\\\n                        record.model_role,\\\\n                        record.assigned_model,\\\\n                        record.assigned_provider,\\\\n                        record.status.value,\\\\n                        record.handoff_doc_path,\\\\n                        record.result_summary,\\\\n                        record.token_used,\\\\n                        record.duration_sec,\\\\n                        record.started_at,\\\\n                        record.finished_at,\\\\n                        record.retry_count,\\\\n                        record.verify_cmd,\\\\n                        json.dumps(record.metadata),\\\\n                    ),\\\\n                )\\\\n                records.append(record)\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (now, task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n        return records\\\\n\\\\n    def count_stages(self, task_id: str) -> int:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM stages WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return int(row[\\\\\\\"count\\\\\\\"])\\\\n\\\\n    def get_stage(self, stage_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\", (stage_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def list_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE task_id = ? ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id,),\\\\n            ).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_stage(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def list_completed_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM stages\\\\n                WHERE task_id = ? AND status IN (?, ?)\\\\n                ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id, StageStatus.DONE.value, StageStatus.SKIPPED.value),\\\\n            ).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_stage(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def next_pending_stage(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM stages\\\\n                WHERE task_id = ? AND status = ?\\\\n                ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\n                LIMIT 1\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id, StageStatus.PENDING.value),\\\\n            ).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def assign_stage_model(self, stage_id: str, model: str, provider: str) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET assigned_model = ?, assigned_provider = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (model, provider, stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def mark_stage_running(self, stage_id: str, handoff_doc_path: str) -> None:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, handoff_doc_path = ?, started_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (StageStatus.RUNNING.value, handoff_doc_path, now, stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def complete_stage(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        stage_id: str,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        result_summary: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        token_used: int = 0,\\\\n        duration_sec: float = 0.0,\\\\n    ) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, token_used = ?, duration_sec = ?,\\\\n                    finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    StageStatus.DONE.value,\\\\n                    result_summary,\\\\n                    token_used,\\\\n                    duration_sec,\\\\n                    utcnow_iso(),\\\\n                    stage_id,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def skip_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (StageStatus.SKIPPED.value, result_summary, utcnow_iso(), stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def reset_stage_to_pending(self, stage_id: str, *, retry_count: Optional[int] = None) -> None:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"UPDATE stages SET status = ?, started_at = NULL\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: list[object] = [StageStatus.PENDING.value]\\\\n        if retry_count is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\", retry_count = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params.append(retry_count)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params.append(stage_id)\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(query, tuple(params))\\\\n\\\\n    def fail_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str], retry_count: int) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, retry_count = ?, finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '260,520p' models/quota_manager.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\n        # Waste urgency: boost accounts with quota about to be lost on reset\\\\n        waste_urgency = 0.0\\\\n        hours = snapshot.time_until_reset_hours\\\\n        if 0 < hours <= self.WASTE_WINDOW_HOURS and remaining > 0:\\\\n            waste_urgency = remaining * (self.WASTE_WINDOW_HOURS - hours) / self.WASTE_WINDOW_HOURS\\\\n\\\\n        # Safety bonus: prefer accounts that can sustain a full task\\\\n        safety = self.SAFETY_BONUS if remaining > self.TASK_SAFETY_THRESHOLD else 0\\\\n\\\\n        # Inertia: preference for staying on current account\\\\n        # Extra inertia when healthy \\u2014 don't switch between two good accounts\\\\n        inertia = 0\\\\n        if is_current:\\\\n            inertia = self.INERTIA_BONUS\\\\n            if remaining > self.TASK_SAFETY_THRESHOLD:\\\\n                inertia += self.HEALTHY_INERTIA\\\\n\\\\n        return remaining + waste_urgency + safety + inertia\\\\n\\\\n    def _is_fresh_reset(self, snapshot: QuotaSnapshot) -> bool:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Check if account just reset or is in immediate post-reset window.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return snapshot.remaining_percent >= self.FRESH_QUOTA_THRESHOLD\\\\n\\\\n    def analyze_and_decide(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        current_email: str,\\\\n        current_snapshot: Optional[QuotaSnapshot],\\\\n        all_snapshots: list[QuotaSnapshot],\\\\n    ) -> SwitchDecision:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        Analyze quota situation and decide whether to switch accounts.\\\\n\\\\n        Uses unified scoring that balances:\\\\n        1. Waste prevention (use expiring quota before it's lost)\\\\n        2. Task safety (prefer >40% for new tasks)\\\\n        3. Continuity (inertia for current account)\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        if not current_snapshot:\\\\n            # No current account info \\u2014 pick the best available\\\\n            if all_snapshots:\\\\n                scored = [(s, self._score_account(s)) for s in all_snapshots]\\\\n                best, best_score = max(scored, key=lambda x: x[1])\\\\n                return SwitchDecision(\\\\n                    should_switch=True,\\\\n                    reason=f\\\\\\\"No current account \\u2014 selecting best (score {best_score:.0f})\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    target_email=best.email,\\\\n                    target_remaining=best.remaining_percent,\\\\n                    current_email=current_email,\\\\n                    current_remaining=None,\\\\n                )\\\\n            return SwitchDecision(\\\\n                should_switch=False,\\\\n                reason=\\\\\\\"No account quota available\\\\\\\",\\\\n                target_email=None,\\\\n                target_remaining=None,\\\\n                current_email=current_email,\\\\n                current_remaining=None,\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        # Score current account\\\\n        current_score = self._score_account(current_snapshot, is_current=True)\\\\n\\\\n        # Score all candidates\\\\n        candidates = [s for s in all_snapshots if s.email != current_email]\\\\n        if not candidates:\\\\n            return SwitchDecision(\\\\n                should_switch=False,\\\\n                reason=\\\\\\\"No other accounts available\\\\\\\",\\\\n                target_email=None,\\\\n                target_remaining=None,\\\\n                current_email=current_email,\\\\n                current_remaining=current_snapshot.remaining_percent,\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        scored = [(s, self._score_account(s)) for s in candidates]\\\\n        best_snapshot, best_score = max(scored, key=lambda x: x[1])\\\\n\\\\n        improvement = best_score - current_score\\\\n\\\\n        # Determine reason for decision\\\\n        if improvement > self.SWITCH_THRESHOLD:\\\\n            # Explain WHY we're switching\\\\n            reason_parts = []\\\\n            best_hours = best_snapshot.time_until_reset_hours\\\\n            if (0 < best_hours <= self.WASTE_WINDOW_HOURS\\\\n                    and best_snapshot.remaining_percent > 0):\\\\n                reason_parts.append(\\\\n                    f\\\\\\\"waste prevention: {best_snapshot.remaining_percent}% expiring in {best_hours:.1f}h\\\\\\\"\\\\n                )\\\\n            if best_snapshot.remaining_percent > self.TASK_SAFETY_THRESHOLD:\\\\n                reason_parts.append(f\\\\\\\"task-safe ({best_snapshot.remaining_percent}% remaining)\\\\\\\")\\\\n            if self._is_fresh_reset(best_snapshot):\\\\n                reason_parts.append(f\\\\\\\"fresh reset ({best_snapshot.remaining_percent}%)\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n            reason = \\\\\\\"; \\\\\\\".join(reason_parts) if reason_parts else f\\\\\\\"better score\\\\\\\"\\\\n            reason += f\\\\\\\" (score {current_score:.0f} \\u2192 {best_score:.0f}, \\u0394{improvement:.0f})\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n            return SwitchDecision(\\\\n                should_switch=True,\\\\n                reason=reason,\\\\n                target_email=best_snapshot.email,\\\\n                target_remaining=best_snapshot.remaining_percent,\\\\n                current_email=current_email,\\\\n                current_remaining=current_snapshot.remaining_percent,\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        # Stay \\u2014 explain why\\\\n        if current_snapshot.remaining_percent > self.TASK_SAFETY_THRESHOLD:\\\\n            reason = f\\\\\\\"Current quota healthy ({current_snapshot.remaining_percent}% remaining)\\\\\\\"\\\\n        else:\\\\n            reason = f\\\\\\\"No significant improvement (\\u0394{improvement:.0f}, need >{self.SWITCH_THRESHOLD})\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        return SwitchDecision(\\\\n            should_switch=False,\\\\n            reason=reason,\\\\n            target_email=None,\\\\n            target_remaining=None,\\\\n            current_email=current_email,\\\\n            current_remaining=current_snapshot.remaining_percent,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    def check_and_switch_if_needed(self) -> SwitchDecision:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        Check all account quotas and switch if necessary.\\\\n\\\\n        Returns: SwitchDecision with details about action taken.\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        now = int(time.time())\\\\n\\\\n        # Rate limit checks (no more than once per check_interval)\\\\n        if now - self.last_check_time < self.check_interval_minutes * 60:\\\\n            return SwitchDecision(\\\\n                should_switch=False,\\\\n                reason=f\\\\\\\"Check rate limited (next in {self.check_interval_minutes}min)\\\\\\\",\\\\n                target_email=None,\\\\n                target_remaining=None,\\\\n                current_email=None,\\\\n                current_remaining=None,\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        self.last_check_time = now\\\\n\\\\n        # Fetch all quotas\\\\n        snapshots = self.fetch_all_quotas()\\\\n        if not snapshots:\\\\n            return SwitchDecision(\\\\n                should_switch=False,\\\\n                reason=\\\\\\\"No accounts found\\\\\\\",\\\\n                target_email=None,\\\\n                target_remaining=None,\\\\n                current_email=None,\\\\n                current_remaining=None,\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        # Determine current active account\\\\n        current_email: Optional[str] = None\\\\n        current_snapshot: Optional[QuotaSnapshot] = None\\\\n\\\\n        # Try to get Codex active account\\\\n        try:\\\\n            codex_email = get_active_codex_email()\\\\n            if codex_email:\\\\n                current_email = codex_email\\\\n        except Exception:\\\\n            pass\\\\n\\\\n        # Try to get Google active account if no Codex\\\\n        if not current_email:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                google_email = get_active_google_email()\\\\n                if google_email:\\\\n                    current_email = google_email\\\\n            except Exception:\\\\n                pass\\\\n\\\\n        # Find current snapshot\\\\n        if current_email:\\\\n            for snapshot in snapshots:\\\\n                if snapshot.email == current_email:\\\\n                    current_snapshot = snapshot\\\\n                    break\\\\n\\\\n        # Analyze and decide\\\\n        decision = self.analyze_and_decide(current_email or \\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\", current_snapshot, snapshots)\\\\n\\\\n        # Execute switch if needed\\\\n        if decision.should_switch and decision.target_email:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                # Determine which provider the target belongs to\\\\n                target_snapshot = next(s for s in snapshots if s.email == decision.target_email)\\\\n\\\\n                if target_snapshot.provider == Provider.CODEX:\\\\n                    set_active_codex_account(decision.target_email)\\\\n                else:  # GOOGLE\\\\n                    set_active_google_account(decision.target_email)\\\\n\\\\n                self.last_switch_time = now\\\\n                decision.reason += \\\\\\\" [SWITCHED]\\\\\\\"\\\\n            except Exception as e:\\\\n                print(f\\\\\\\"Switch error: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n                decision.reason += f\\\\\\\" [SWITCH FAILED: {e}]\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        return decision\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef print_quota_report(snapshots: list[QuotaSnapshot], manager: Optional[QuotaManager] = None) -> None:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Print formatted quota report for all accounts.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not snapshots:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"No accounts found.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return\\\\n\\\\n    w = 120\\\\n    print()\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\u2554\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"\\u2550\\\\\\\" * w + \\\\\\\"\\u2557\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\u2551 QUOTA STATUS REPORT\\\\\\\".ljust(w + 2) + \\\\\\\"\\u2551\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\u2560\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"\\u2550\\\\\\\" * w + \\\\\\\"\\u2563\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print(\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\u2551 \\\\\\\"\\\\n        + \\\\\\\"Email\\\\\\\".ljust(40)\\\\n        + \\\\\\\" Plan   Remaining           Reset Time            Until\\\\\\\"\\\\n        + \\\\\\\"  Score\\\\\\\"\\\\n        + \\\\\\\" Provider \\u2551\\\\\\\"\\\\n    )\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\u255f\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"\\u2500\\\\\\\" * w + \\\\\\\"\\u2562\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    for snapshot in sorted(snapshots, key=lambda s: (-s.remaining_percent, s.time_until_reset_hours)):\\\\n        remaining = snapshot.remaining_percent\\\\n        filled = int(remaining / 5)\\\\n        bar = \\\\\\\"[\\\\\\\" + (\\\\\\\"\\u2588\\\\\\\" * filled) + (\\\\\\\"\\u2591\\\\\\\" * (20 - filled)) + \\\\\\\"]\\\\\\\"\\\\n        icon = \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udfe2\\\\\\\" if remaining > 40 else \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udfe1\\\\\\\" if remaining > 5 else \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd34\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        score_str = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        if manager:\\\\n            score = manager._score_account(snapshot)\\\\n            score_str = f\\\\\\\"{score:>6.1f}\\\\\\\"\\\\n        else:\\\\n            score_str = \\\\\\\"    - \\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        line = (\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"\\u2551 {icon} {snapshot.email:<38} {snapshot.plan_type:<6} \\\\\\\"\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"{bar} {remaining:>3}% \\\\\\\"\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"{snapshot.reset_at_readable:<19} {snapshot.time_until_reset_hours:>5.1f}h \\\\\\\"\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"{score_str}\\\\\\\"\\\\n            f\\\\\\\" {snapshot.provider.value:>8} \\u2551\\\\\\\"\\\\n        )\\\\n        print(line)\\\\n\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\u255a\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"\\u2550\\\\\\\" * w + \\\\\\\"\\u255d\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nif __name__ == \\\\\\\"__main__\\\\\\\":\\\\n    manager = return os.environ.get(name, default)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _get_env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:\\\\n    value = os.environ.get(name)\\\\n    if value is None:\\\\n        return default\\\\n    try:\\\\n        return int(value)\\\\n    except ValueError:\\\\n        return default\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _get_env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:\\\\n    value = os.environ.get(name)\\\\n    if value is None:\\\\n        return default\\\\n    try:\\\\n        return float(value)\\\\n    except ValueError:\\\\n        return default\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _get_env_bool(name: str, default: bool) -> bool:\\\\n    value = os.environ.get(name)\\\\n    if value is None:\\\\n        return default\\\\n    return value.strip().lower() in {\\\\\\\"1\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"true\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"yes\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"on\\\\\\\"}\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nCLAUDE_PORT = _get_env_int(\\\\\\\"CLAUDE_PORT\\\\\\\", 8001)\\\\nCODEX_PORT = _get_env_int(\\\\\\\"CODEX_PORT\\\\\\\", 8002)\\\\nGEMINI_PORT = -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,280p' scripts/orchestra_cli.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"#!/usr/bin/env python3\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Interactive CLI for running multi-agent orchestrator workflows.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport argparse\\\\nimport asyncio\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport logging\\\\nimport re\\\\nimport sys\\\\nimport time\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\n\\\\nimport uuid\\\\nimport yaml\\\\n\\\\n# Fix path to allow importing from the project root if run from anywhere\\\\nsys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.absolute()))\\\\n\\\\nfrom common.acp_client import ACPClient\\\\nfrom common.models import Message, MessagePart, RunStatus\\\\nfrom scripts.check_server_status import check_all_servers, StatusReport\\\\nfrom orchestrator.pipeline import PipelineDefinition, PipelineExecutor, PipelineError\\\\nfrom orchestrator.stage import StageDefinition, StageResult\\\\n\\\\n# Setup minimal logging, only warning+ so it doesn't clutter CLI\\\\nlogging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, format=\\\\\\\"%(message)s\\\\\\\")\\\\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef hide_code_blocks(text: str) -> str:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Replaces markdown code blocks and diffs with a hidden placeholder.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not text:\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    # Regex to match ```language \\\\\\\\n code... \\\\\\\\n ```\\\\n    pattern = re.compile(r\\\\\\\"```[a-zA-Z0-9_-]*\\\\\\\\n[\\\\\\\\s\\\\\\\\S]*?\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\", re.MULTILINE)\\\\n    return pattern.sub(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n[CODE DIFF/BLOCK HIDDEN]\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\", text)\\\\n    \\\\n\\\\nclass CLIExecutor(PipelineExecutor):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Custom executor that streams updates to the CLI interface and routes to ACP services.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    \\\\n    def __init__(self, pipeline_def: PipelineDefinition, config_path: str):\\\\n        super().__init__(pipeline_def)\\\\n        self.session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]\\\\n        self.clients: dict[str, ACPClient] = {}\\\\n        \\\\n        with open(config_path, \\\\\\\"r\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\") as handle:\\\\n            data = yaml.safe_load(handle) or {}\\\\n            for name, info in data.get(\\\\\\\"agents\\\\\\\", {}).items():\\\\n                self.clients[name] = ACPClient(info[\\\\\\\"base_url\\\\\\\"])\\\\n                \\\\n    async def cleanup(self):\\\\n        import asyncio\\\\n        await asyncio.gather(*(client.close() for client in self.clients.values()), return_exceptions=True)\\\\n\\\\n    async def _invoke_model(self, stage_def: StageDefinition, prompt: str) -> str:\\\\n        agent_name = stage_def.metadata.get(\\\\\\\"agent\\\\\\\")\\\\n        if not agent_name:\\\\n            return await super()._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            \\\\n        client = self.clients.get(agent_name)\\\\n        if not client:\\\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\\\\\"Unknown agent '{agent_name}' configured in metadata for stage {stage_def.stage_id}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        message = Message(\\\\n            role=\\\\\\\"user\\\\\\\",\\\\n            parts=[MessagePart(content=prompt)]\\\\n        )\\\\n        \\\\n        run = await client.create_run(agent_name, [message], session_id=self.session_id)\\\\n        if run.status != RunStatus.COMPLETED:\\\\n            raise RuntimeError(f\\\\\\\"Agent {agent_name} failed: {run.error}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        if not run.output_messages:\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return run.output_messages[0].text\\\\n    \\\\n    async def _execute_stage(self, stage_def: StageDefinition) -> StageResult:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Override to provide CLI feedback before and after the stage.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd04 CURRENT STEP: {stage_def.name} ({stage_def.stage_id})\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83e\\udd16 Agent Model:  {stage_def.model}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        # Prepare prompt to show user what is being asked\\\\n        prompt = self._prepare_prompt(stage_def)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Input] -> Sending to Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        # We don't hide code blocks in prompt for context, but we can limit size\\\\n        short_prompt = prompt if len(prompt) < 1000 else prompt[:500] + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n...[TRUNCATED FOR DISPLAY]...\\\\\\\" + prompt[-500:]\\\\n        print(short_prompt)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u23f3 Waiting for agent response...\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        start_time = time.time()\\\\n        try:\\\\n            output = await self._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            \\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 Agent responded in {execution_time:.1f}s\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Output] <- Received from Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            # Hide code blocks for clean UI\\\\n            clean_output = hide_code_blocks(output)\\\\n            print(clean_output)\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            return StageResult(\\\\n                stage_id=stage_def.stage_id,\\\\n                status=\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\",\\\\n                output=output,\\\\n                raw_output=output,\\\\n                execution_time=execution_time,\\\\n                model_used=stage_def.model,\\\\n            )\\\\n            \\\\n        except Exception as exc:\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Stage failed after {execution_time:.1f}s: {exc}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            raise  # Let the base class intercept it and handle fail_fast\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def ensure_servers_running(config_path: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> bool:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Check if required servers are healthy. Return True if all ok.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd0d Checking ACP Server Status...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    report = await check_all_servers(config_path, timeout)\\\\n    if report.unhealthy_count == 0:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 All {report.total_servers} servers are healthy!\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return True\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c SOME SERVERS ARE UNREACHABLE!\\\\\\\")\\\\n    for server in report.servers:\\\\n        if not server.healthy:\\\\n            err = f\\\\\\\" ({server.error})\\\\\\\" if server.error else \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  - {server.base_url} is DOWN{err}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nPlease start the required servers (e.g. using scripts/run_*_server.py).\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return False\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef get_available_workflows() -> list[Path]:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Return a list of workflow YAML files.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    workflows_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / \\\\\\\"workflows\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not workflows_dir.exists():\\\\n        return []\\\\n    return sorted(workflows_dir.glob(\\\\\\\"*.yaml\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def main_async(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\\\n    # 1. Check Servers\\\\n    servers_ok = await ensure_servers_running(args.config, args.timeout)\\\\n    if not servers_ok:\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"               ORCHESTRA MULTI-AGENT CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60 + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # 2. Select Workflow\\\\n    workflows = get_available_workflows()\\\\n    if not workflows:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c No workflows found in the 'workflows/' directory.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    workflow_path = None\\\\n    if args.workflow:\\\\n        workflow_path = Path(args.workflow)\\\\n        if not workflow_path.exists():\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Workflow {workflow_path} not found.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n    else:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"Available Workflows:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        for idx, wf in enumerate(workflows):\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  [{idx + 1}] {wf.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                choice = input(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nSelect a workflow (1-%d): \\\\\\\" % len(workflows))\\\\n                idx = int(choice.strip()) - 1\\\\n                if 0 <= idx < len(workflows):\\\\n                    workflow_path = workflows[idx]\\\\n                    break\\\\n                else:\\\\n                    print(\\\\\\\"Invalid choice.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except ValueError:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"Please enter a number.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n                \\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u2705 Selected workflow: {workflow_path.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # Load pipeline\\\\n    try:\\\\n        pipeline_def = PipelineDefinition(workflow_path)\\\\n    except PipelineError as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Failed to load pipeline: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    # 3. Get Task\\\\n    task_description = args.task\\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nEnter task description (press Enter twice to finish):\\\\\\\")\\\\n        lines = []\\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                line = input()\\\\n                if not line and (not lines or not lines[-1]):\\\\n                    break\\\\n                lines.append(line)\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n        task_description = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(lines).strip()\\\\n        \\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c Task description cannot be empty.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n\\\\n    # 4. Execute Pipeline\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\ud83d\\ude80 Starting Pipeline Execution...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = CLIExecutor(pipeline_def, args.config)\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        # Also map user_task so it's available for workflows explicitly requesting it\\\\n        initial_state = {\\\\\\\"user_task\\\\\\\": task_description}\\\\n        results = await executor.execute(task_description, initial_state=initial_state)\\\\n        \\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83c\\udf89 PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETED\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        for stage_id, result in results.items():\\\\n            icon = \\\\\\\"\\u2705\\\\\\\" if result.status == \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"\\u274c\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"{icon} Stage '{stage_id}': {result.status.upper()} ({result.execution_time:.1f}s)\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        # Check for absolute failure\\\\n        if any(r.status == \\\\\\\"failed\\\\\\\" for r in results.values()):\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline completed with ERRORS.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n            \\\\n    except Exception as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline execution failed due to an error: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        import traceback\\\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n    finally:\\\\n        await executor.cleanup()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef main() -> None:\\\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\\\\\"Interactive Multi-Agent Orchestrator CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--config\\\\\\\", default=\\\\\\\"config/agents.yaml\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to agents.yaml\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--timeout\\\\\\\", type=float, default=3.0, help=\\\\\\\"Per-server health check timeout in seconds\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--workflow\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to a specific workflow YAML, bypasses selection\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--task\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Initial task description, bypasses input dialog\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        asyncio.run(main_async(args))\\\\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExiting CLI...\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(0)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nif __name__ == \\\\\\\"__main__\\\\\\\":\\\\n    main()\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_23\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' harness/db.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"SQLite schema and connection helpers for the harness.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport sqlite3\\\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nSCHEMA = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nPRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (\\\\n    task_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    title TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    description TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    goal TEXT,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    complexity TEXT DEFAULT 'medium',\\\\n    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    working_dir TEXT,\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stages (\\\\n    stage_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(task_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    stage_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    stage_order INTEGER NOT NULL,\\\\n    model_role TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    assigned_model TEXT,\\\\n    assigned_provider TEXT,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    handoff_doc_path TEXT,\\\\n    result_summary TEXT,\\\\n    token_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    duration_sec REAL DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    started_at TEXT,\\\\n    finished_at TEXT,\\\\n    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}'\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permission_requests (\\\\n    request_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    stage_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES stages(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    action TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    context TEXT,\\\\n    risk_level TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    decision TEXT,\\\\n    voters TEXT DEFAULT '[]',\\\\n    decided_at TEXT\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quota_events (\\\\n    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    provider TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    account_email TEXT,\\\\n    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status_priority\\\\n    ON tasks(status, priority DESC, created_at ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_order\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_status\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, status, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass HarnessDB:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Small wrapper around a SQLite database file.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:\\\\n        self.db_path = Path(db_path)\\\\n        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n        self.initialize()\\\\n\\\\n    def initialize(self) -> None:\\\\n        with self.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.executescript(SCHEMA)\\\\n\\\\n    @contextmanager\\\\n    def connect(self) -> Iterator[sqlite3.Connection]:\\\\n        conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)\\\\n        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\\\\n        conn.execute(\\\\\\\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\\\\\\\")\\\\n        try:\\\\n            yield conn\\\\n            conn.commit()\\\\n        except Exception:\\\\n            conn.rollback()\\\\n            raise\\\\n        finally:\\\\n            conn.close()\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_24\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash tests/harness/test_stage_executor.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_24\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' tests/harness/test_stage_executor.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"import asyncio\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.models import PermissionDecision, RiskLevel, StageRecord, StageStatus\\\\nfrom harness.permission_gate import PermissionGate\\\\nfrom harness.stage_executor import CommandExecution, PermissionBlockedError, StageExecutor\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass DummyPermissionGate:\\\\n    def __init__(self, decision):\\\\n        self.decision = decision\\\\n\\\\n    async def decide(self, command, **kwargs):\\\\n        return self.decision\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass DummyVoting:\\\\n    def __init__(self, decision):\\\\n        self.decision = decision\\\\n\\\\n    async def vote(self, **kwargs):\\\\n        return self.decision\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def _runner(command, cwd):\\\\n    return CommandExecution(returncode=0, stdout=\\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\", stderr=\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef make_stage(provider: str, metadata=None) -> StageRecord:\\\\n    return StageRecord(\\\\n        stage_id=\\\\\\\"stage\\\\\\\",\\\\n        task_id=\\\\\\\"task\\\\\\\",\\\\n        stage_type=\\\\\\\"github_ops\\\\\\\" if provider == \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"code\\\\\\\",\\\\n        stage_order=1,\\\\n        model_role=\\\\\\\"github_ops\\\\\\\" if provider == \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"coder\\\\\\\",\\\\n        assigned_model=\\\\\\\"gpt-4o\\\\\\\" if provider == \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"gpt-5.4-codex\\\\\\\",\\\\n        assigned_provider=provider,\\\\n        status=StageStatus.PENDING,\\\\n        handoff_doc_path=None,\\\\n        result_summary=None,\\\\n        token_used=0,\\\\n        duration_sec=0.0,\\\\n        started_at=None,\\\\n        finished_at=None,\\\\n        metadata=metadata or {},\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_stage_executor_runs_github_commands(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    handoff = tmp_path / \\\\\\\"handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n    handoff.write_text(\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = StageExecutor(\\\\n        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\\\\n            PermissionDecision(risk_level=RiskLevel.MODERATE, decision=\\\\\\\"auto_approved\\\\\\\", reason=\\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\")\\\\n        ),\\\\n        runner=_runner,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    result = asyncio.run(\\\\n        executor.execute(\\\\n            make_stage(\\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\", {\\\\\\\"commands\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\"echo pushed\\\\\\\"]}),\\\\n            str(handoff),\\\\n            str(tmp_path),\\\\n        )\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    assert result.status == StageStatus.DONE\\\\n    assert result.summary == \\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_stage_executor_blocks_unapproved_command(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    handoff = tmp_path / \\\\\\\"handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n    handoff.write_text(\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = StageExecutor(\\\\n        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\\\\n            PermissionDecision(\\\\n                risk_level=RiskLevel.DANGEROUS,\\\\n                decision=\\\\\\\"needs_user_approval\\\\\\\",\\\\n                reason=\\\\\\\"blocked\\\\\\\",\\\\n            )\\\\n        ),\\\\n        runner=_runner,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    try:\\\\n        asyncio.run(executor.run_verify_cmd(\\\\\\\"git push origin main\\\\\\\", str(tmp_path)))\\\\n    except PermissionBlockedError as exc:\\\\n        assert \\\\\\\"blocked\\\\\\\" in str(exc)\\\\n        assert \\\\\\\"git push origin main\\\\\\\" in exc.command\\\\n    else:\\\\n        raise AssertionError(\\\\\\\"Expected PermissionBlockedError\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_stage_executor_checks_raw_verify_command(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    from harness.models import ModelVote, VoteResult\\\\n\\\\n    async def user_approver(command: str, context: str):\\\\n        return False\\\\n\\\\n    executor = StageExecutor(\\\\n        permission_gate=PermissionGate(\\\\n            voting=DummyVoting(\\\\n                VoteResult(\\\\n                    decision=\\\\\\\"REJECT\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    votes=[ModelVote(model=\\\\\\\"gpt-5.4-codex\\\\\\\", vote=\\\\\\\"REJECT\\\\\\\", reason=\\\\\\\"blocked\\\\\\\")],\\\\n                    unanimous=True,\\\\n                    needs_escalation=True,\\\\n                )\\\\n            ),\\\\n            user_approver=user_approver,\\\\n        ),\\\\n        runner=_runner,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    try:\\\\n        asyncio.run(executor.run_verify_cmd(\\\\\\\"git push origin main\\\\\\\", str(tmp_path)))\\\\n    except PermissionBlockedError as exc:\\\\n        assert exc.command == \\\\\\\"git push origin main\\\\\\\"\\\\n    else:\\\\n        raise AssertionError(\\\\\\\"Expected PermissionBlockedError\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_25\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,240p' artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Output: plan\\\\n## Summary\\\\nImplement the robustness work in three slices, keeping `QuotaRouter` selection pure and pushing all operational side effects into the harness runtime / CLI layer.\\\\n\\\\n1. Codex account rotation\\\\n- Keep `QuotaRouter.select_model()` and `can_run_stage()` side-effect free.\\\\n- Change Codex candidate selection from \\\\\\\"best provider snapshot\\\\\\\" to \\\\\\\"best eligible Codex account snapshot\\\\\\\", using the existing use-it-or-lose-it score.\\\\n- Preserve the chosen `account_email` on the selected stage, preferably in stage metadata if the DB schema should stay stable.\\\\n- Add an explicit pre-execution Codex activation step that calls `models.codex.account.get_active_email()` and `set_active_account()` only when the chosen account differs.\\\\n\\\\n2. Daemon lifecycle\\\\n- Use the harness CLI in [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) as the management entrypoint, not the older workflow CLI in [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py).\\\\n- Add `daemon start`, `daemon stop`, `daemon status`, plus a hidden worker subcommand that runs `Harness.run_forever()`.\\\\n- Prefer a portable detached `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` / nohup-style path for tests instead of systemd-specific logic.\\\\n- Persist PID metadata, detect stale PID files, and handle `SIGTERM` so the loop exits cleanly after the current iteration.\\\\n\\\\n3. Logging, monitoring, and dashboard\\\\n- Add centralized logging setup for harness CLI + daemon startup.\\\\n- Emit structured JSONL stage events for start, success, retry, quota pause, permission pause, and terminal failure.\\\\n- Reuse persisted `stages.token_used` and `stages.duration_sec` for aggregates; parse provider output for token usage where available and leave `0` when unavailable.\\\\n- Add a `dashboard` command that combines DB state, quota/permission audit tables, daemon state, and recent JSONL events into one status summary.\\\\n\\\\n## Key File Targets\\\\n- [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py)\\\\n- [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py)\\\\n- [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py)\\\\n- [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py)\\\\n- [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py)\\\\n- [config/settings.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/config/settings.py)\\\\n- New modules are justified for daemon management and telemetry aggregation.\\\\n\\\\n## Risks To Avoid\\\\n- Do not let quota feasibility checks mutate global Codex auth state.\\\\n- Do not add daemon logic to the legacy orchestrator CLI; keep it on the harness path.\\\\n- Do not rely on live systemd in tests; use deterministic unit seams around PID files, signals, and subprocess launch.\\\\n\\\\n## Test Plan\\\\n- Extend router tests for multi-account Codex ranking and no-side-effect feasibility checks.\\\\n- Add harness tests for Codex account activation before a Codex stage executes.\\\\n- Add daemon tests for PID lifecycle, stale PID cleanup, and graceful stop/status behavior.\\\\n- Add telemetry/dashboard tests for JSONL emission and aggregate summaries.\\\\n- Final verification: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\\\\n\\\\n## Baseline\\\\nCurrent baseline is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` passed with `21 passed`.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_33\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/02_code_handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_33\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/02_code_handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Handoff: plan -> code\\\\n## Task\\\\nPhase 4: \\u5065\\u58ee\\u6027 for code-orchestra harness\\\\n\\\\n## Context Gathered\\\\n- `QuotaRouter` currently collapses each provider to a single best snapshot in [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py), which is not enough for Codex multi-account rotation.\\\\n- `Harness._run_stage()` in [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) persists model/provider selection but has no Codex account activation, no daemon hooks, and no telemetry hooks.\\\\n- `models.codex.account.set_active_account()` already handles token refresh + auth file switching in [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py); the harness should integrate it as a runtime step, not from the router.\\\\n- `StageExecutionResult` already has `token_used` and `duration_sec`, and the stages table already persists both via [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py).\\\\n- Baseline verification is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` => `21 passed`.\\\\n\\\\n## Recommended Implementation Order\\\\n1. Codex rotation\\\\n- Update router eligibility/fallback logic so Codex models consider all eligible Codex snapshots, keeping the chosen `account_email`.\\\\n- Persist the selected account on the stage.\\\\n- Add a small helper in the harness path to activate the selected Codex account just before execution.\\\\n- Add tests first for router ranking, then for harness-side activation.\\\\n\\\\n2. Daemon mode\\\\n- Add a dedicated daemon helper module with PID file read/write, process liveness checks, start/stop/status, and stale PID cleanup.\\\\n- Extend the harness parser with `daemon` and a hidden worker command.\\\\n- Add `SIGTERM`-aware stop handling around `run_forever`, ideally with an `asyncio.Event` / shutdown flag seam that is easy to unit test.\\\\n\\\\n3. Telemetry + dashboard\\\\n- Add centralized logging config and a JSONL event writer module.\\\\n- Emit stage lifecycle events from the harness runtime, not from the router.\\\\n- Add a dashboard summary builder that reads DB state + event log + daemon status.\\\\n- Expose it via `dashboard` in the harness CLI.\\\\n\\\\n## Concrete Guidance\\\\n- Prefer stage metadata for the selected Codex account instead of widening the DB schema unless a wider schema clearly improves readability.\\\\n- Keep `can_run_stage()` pure; it is called from paused-task recovery and should not switch accounts.\\\\n- Parse token usage opportunistically in [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py); do not block this phase on provider-specific token support.\\\\n- Treat [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py) as unrelated legacy workflow UI unless you discover a hard requirement otherwise.\\\\n\\\\n## Verification Target\\\\nRun `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` after each slice if possible, then once at the end.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.updated\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_27\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"todo_list\\\",\\\"items\\\":[{\\\"text\\\":\\\"Inspect QuotaRouter, Harness scheduler loop, Codex account management, DB/task models, and current CLI entrypoints\\\",\\\"completed\\\":true},{\\\"text\\\":\\\"Confirm current harness test baseline and identify extension points for daemon, telemetry, and dashboard\\\",\\\"completed\\\":true},{\\\"text\\\":\\\"Write concise plan output and code-stage handoff for the next stage\\\",\\\"completed\\\":true}]}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_34\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\u8ba1\\u5212\\u9636\\u6bb5\\u5df2\\u5b8c\\u6210\\uff0c\\u5b9e\\u65bd handoff \\u5df2\\u5199\\u5165 [01_plan_output.md](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/01_plan_output.md) \\u548c [02_code_handoff.md](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/02_code_handoff.md)\\u3002\\\\n\\\\n\\u672c\\u9636\\u6bb5\\u6ca1\\u6709\\u6539\\u4e1a\\u52a1\\u4ee3\\u7801\\uff0c\\u53ea\\u505a\\u4e86\\u4ee3\\u7801\\u8def\\u5f84\\u68b3\\u7406\\u3001\\u5b9e\\u73b0\\u5207\\u5206\\u548c\\u4e0b\\u4e00\\u9636\\u6bb5\\u6307\\u5f15\\u3002\\u57fa\\u7ebf\\u9a8c\\u8bc1\\u5df2\\u8dd1\\uff1a`python3 Codex \\u591a\\u8d26\\u53f7\\u9009\\u62e9\\u4e0e\\u6fc0\\u6d3b\\u5206\\u79bb\\uff0c\\u518d\\u52a0 daemon \\u751f\\u547d\\u5468\\u671f\\uff0c\\u6700\\u540e\\u8865 telemetry/dashboard {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' harness/quota_router.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"Quota-aware model routing.\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nimport yaml\n\nfrom config import settings\nfrom harness.chat2api_client import Chat2APIClient\nfrom harness.models import ModelChoice, StageRecord, TaskRecord\nfrom models.quota_manager import QuotaManager, QuotaSnapshot\n\n\nclass AllQuotaExhaustedError(RuntimeError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when no configured candidate model is currently usable.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass QuotaRouter:\n    \"\"\"Select a model using role preferences, availability, and quota heuristics.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        catalog_path: str | Path = settings.HARNESS_ROLE_MODELS_PATH,\n        *,\n        quota_manager: Optional[QuotaManager] = None,\n        chat_client: Optional[Chat2APIClient] = None,\n        min_remaining_pct: int = settings.HARNESS_MIN_QUOTA_PCT,\n        enable_llm_selector: bool = True,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.catalog_path = Path(catalog_path)\n        self.quota_manager = quota_manager or QuotaManager()\n        self.chat_client = chat_client or Chat2APIClient()\n        self.min_remaining_pct = min_remaining_pct\n        self.enable_llm_selector = enable_llm_selector\n        self.catalog = self._load_catalog()\n\n    def _load_catalog(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        with open(self.catalog_path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as handle:\n            return yaml.safe_load(handle) or {}\n\n    def models_for_role(self, role: str) -> list[str]:\n        roles = self.catalog.get(\"roles\", {})\n        configured = roles.get(role)\n        if configured:\n            return list(configured)\n        return list(self.catalog.get(\"models\", {}).keys())\n\n    def get_model_info(self, model: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return dict(self.catalog.get(\"models\", {}).get(model, {}))\n\n    def chat2api_id(self, model: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the chat2api model ID for a catalog model name.\"\"\"\n        info = self.get_model_info(model)\n        return info.get(\"chat2api_id\", model)\n\n    def list_available_models(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return catalog model names whose chat2api_id is live.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            live_ids = set(self.chat_client.list_models())\n        except Exception:\n            return list(self.catalog.get(\"models\", {}).keys())\n        if not live_ids:\n            return list(self.catalog.get(\"models\", {}).keys())\n        return [\n            name\n            for name in self.catalog.get(\"models\", {})\n            if self.chat2api_id(name) in live_ids\n        ]\n\n    def can_run_stage(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        *,\n        quota_snapshot: Optional[list[QuotaSnapshot]] = None,\n        available_models: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    ) -> bool:\n        try:\n            self.select_model(task, stage, quota_snapshot=quota_snapshot, available_models=available_models)\n        except AllQuotaExhaustedError:\n            return False\n        return True\n\n    def select_model(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        *,\n        quota_snapshot: Optional[list[QuotaSnapshot]] = None,\n        available_models: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    ) -> ModelChoice:\n        quotas = quota_snapshot if quota_snapshot is not None else self.quota_manager.fetch_all_quotas()\n        available = set(available_models or self.list_available_models())\n        eligible = self._eligible_candidates(self.models_for_role(stage.model_role), quotas, available)\n\n        if not eligible:\n            raise AllQuotaExhaustedError(\n                f\"No eligible model for role={stage.model_role} with min_remaining={self.min_remaining_pct}%\"\n            )\n\n        if self.enable_llm_selector:\n            llm_choice = self._select_with_llm(task, stage, quotas, eligible)\n            if llm_choice is not None:\n                return llm_choice\n\n        return self._fallback_select(task, stage, eligible)\n\n    def _eligible_candidates(\n        self,\n        candidates: list[str],\n        quotas: list[QuotaSnapshot],\n        available: set[str],\n    ) -> list[tuple[str, str, Optional[QuotaSnapshot]]]:\n        eligible: list[tuple[str, str, Optional[QuotaSnapshot]]] = []\n        for model in candidates:\n            if model not in available:\n                continue\n            provider = self.get_model_info(model).get(\"provider\")\n            if not provider:\n                continue\n\n            provider_snapshots = [\n                snapshot\n                for snapshot in quotas\n                if snapshot.provider.value == provider and snapshot.remaining_percent >= self.min_remaining_pct\n            ]\n            if provider_snapshots:\n                eligible.append((model, provider, max(provider_snapshots, key=self._score_snapshot)))\n            elif provider == \"github\":\n                eligible.append((model, provider, None))\n        return eligible\n\n    def _select_with_llm(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        quotas: list[QuotaSnapshot],\n        eligible: list[tuple[str, str, Optional[QuotaSnapshot]]],\n    ) -> ModelChoice | None:\n        selector_model = self._pick_selector_model()\n        if selector_model is None:\n            return None\n\n        prompt = self._build_selection_prompt(task, stage, quotas, eligible)\n        try:\n            raw = self.chat_client.chat(\n                model=selector_model,\n                prompt=prompt,\n                system=(\n                    \"You are code-orchestra's model router. \"\n                    \"Return compact JSON with fields model, provider, account, reason.\"\n                ),\n            )\n            parsed = json.loads(raw)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n        chosen_model = parsed.get(\"model\")\n        if not isinstance(chosen_model, str):\n            return None\n\n        eligible_models = {model: (provider, snapshot) for model, provider, snapshot in eligible}\n        selected = eligible_models.get(chosen_model)\n        if selected is None:\n            return None\n\n        provider, snapshot = selected\n        if snapshot is not None:\n            requested_account = parsed.get(\"account\")\n            if isinstance(requested_account, str) and requested_account and requested_account != snapshot.email:\n                alternative = next(\n                    (\n                        quota\n                        for quota in quotas\n                        if quota.provider.value == provider\n                        and quota.email == requested_account\n                        and quota.remaining_percent >= self.min_remaining_pct\n                    ),\n                    None,\n                )\n                if alternative is not None:\n                    snapshot = alternative\n\n        return ModelChoice(\n            model=chosen_model,\n            provider=provider,\n            account_email=snapshot.email if snapshot is not None else None,\n            reason=str(parsed.get(\"reason\") or \"llm_selector\"),\n        )\n\n    def _fallback_select(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        eligible: list[tuple[str, str, Optional[QuotaSnapshot]]],\n    ) -> ModelChoice:\n        best_choice: ModelChoice | None = None\n        best_score: float | None = None\n\n        for role_index, (model, provider, snapshot) in enumerate(eligible):\n            role_bonus = max(0, 40 - role_index * 10)\n            if snapshot is not None:\n                total_score = role_bonus + self._score_snapshot(snapshot)\n                choice = ModelChoice(\n                    model=model,\n                    provider=provider,\n                    account_email=snapshot.email,\n                    reason=(\n                        f\"fallback: role={stage.model_role}, complexity={task.complexity}, \"\n                        f\"remaining={snapshot.remaining_percent}%, \"\n                        f\"reset_in={snapshot.time_until_reset_hours:.1f}h\"\n                    ),\n                )\n            else:\n                total_score = role_bonus + self._score_provider_without_snapshot(provider)\n                choice = ModelChoice(\n                    model=model,\n                    provider=provider,\n                    account_email=None,\n                    reason=f\"fallback: role={stage.model_role}, complexity={task.complexity}, provider={provider}\",\n                )\n\n            if best_score is None or total_score > best_score:\n                best_score = total_score\n                best_choice = choice\n\n        if best_choice is None:\n            raise AllQuotaExhaustedError(\n                f\"No eligible model for role={stage.model_role} with min_remaining={self.min_remaining_pct}%\"\n            )\n        return best_choice\n\n    def _pick_selector_model(self) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Pick the cheapest available model to use as the LLM selector.\n\n        Returns the *chat2api_id* since the selector call goes directly to chat2api.\n        \"\"\"\n        preferred = (\"gpt-4o\", \"claude-haiku-4-5\", \"gemini-3.1-pro\", \"gpt-5.4-codex\")\n        available = set(self.list_available_models())\n        for model in preferred:\n            if model in available:\n                return self.chat2api_id(model)\n        return None\n\n    def _build_selection_prompt(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        quotas: list[QuotaSnapshot],\n        eligible: list[tuple[str, str, Optional[QuotaSnapshot]]],\n    ) -> str:\n        lines = [\n            f\"Task: {task.title}\",\n            f\"Description: {task.description}\",\n            f\"Complexity: {task.complexity}\",\n            f\"Stage: {stage.stage_type} ({stage.model_role})\",\n            \"\",\n            \"Eligible models:\",\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
