-lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,280p' scripts/orchestra_cli.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"#!/usr/bin/env python3\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Interactive CLI for running multi-agent orchestrator workflows.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport argparse\\\\nimport asyncio\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport logging\\\\nimport re\\\\nimport sys\\\\nimport time\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\n\\\\nimport uuid\\\\nimport yaml\\\\n\\\\n# Fix path to allow importing from the project root if run from anywhere\\\\nsys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.absolute()))\\\\n\\\\nfrom common.acp_client import ACPClient\\\\nfrom common.models import Message, MessagePart, RunStatus\\\\nfrom scripts.check_server_status import check_all_servers, StatusReport\\\\nfrom orchestrator.pipeline import PipelineDefinition, PipelineExecutor, PipelineError\\\\nfrom orchestrator.stage import StageDefinition, StageResult\\\\n\\\\n# Setup minimal logging, only warning+ so it doesn't clutter CLI\\\\nlogging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, format=\\\\\\\"%(message)s\\\\\\\")\\\\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef hide_code_blocks(text: str) -> str:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Replaces markdown code blocks and diffs with a hidden placeholder.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not text:\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    # Regex to match ```language \\\\\\\\n code... \\\\\\\\n ```\\\\n    pattern = re.compile(r\\\\\\\"```[a-zA-Z0-9_-]*\\\\\\\\n[\\\\\\\\s\\\\\\\\S]*?\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\", re.MULTILINE)\\\\n    return pattern.sub(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n[CODE DIFF/BLOCK HIDDEN]\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\", text)\\\\n    \\\\n\\\\nclass CLIExecutor(PipelineExecutor):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Custom executor that streams updates to the CLI interface and routes to ACP services.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    \\\\n    def __init__(self, pipeline_def: PipelineDefinition, config_path: str):\\\\n        super().__init__(pipeline_def)\\\\n        self.session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]\\\\n        self.clients: dict[str, ACPClient] = {}\\\\n        \\\\n        with open(config_path, \\\\\\\"r\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\") as handle:\\\\n            data = yaml.safe_load(handle) or {}\\\\n            for name, info in data.get(\\\\\\\"agents\\\\\\\", {}).items():\\\\n                self.clients[name] = ACPClient(info[\\\\\\\"base_url\\\\\\\"])\\\\n                \\\\n    async def cleanup(self):\\\\n        import asyncio\\\\n        await asyncio.gather(*(client.close() for client in self.clients.values()), return_exceptions=True)\\\\n\\\\n    async def _invoke_model(self, stage_def: StageDefinition, prompt: str) -> str:\\\\n        agent_name = stage_def.metadata.get(\\\\\\\"agent\\\\\\\")\\\\n        if not agent_name:\\\\n            return await super()._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            \\\\n        client = self.clients.get(agent_name)\\\\n        if not client:\\\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\\\\\"Unknown agent '{agent_name}' configured in metadata for stage {stage_def.stage_id}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        message = Message(\\\\n            role=\\\\\\\"user\\\\\\\",\\\\n            parts=[MessagePart(content=prompt)]\\\\n        )\\\\n        \\\\n        run = await client.create_run(agent_name, [message], session_id=self.session_id)\\\\n        if run.status != RunStatus.COMPLETED:\\\\n            raise RuntimeError(f\\\\\\\"Agent {agent_name} failed: {run.error}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        if not run.output_messages:\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return run.output_messages[0].text\\\\n    \\\\n    async def _execute_stage(self, stage_def: StageDefinition) -> StageResult:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Override to provide CLI feedback before and after the stage.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd04 CURRENT STEP: {stage_def.name} ({stage_def.stage_id})\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83e\\udd16 Agent Model:  {stage_def.model}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        # Prepare prompt to show user what is being asked\\\\n        prompt = self._prepare_prompt(stage_def)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Input] -> Sending to Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        # We don't hide code blocks in prompt for context, but we can limit size\\\\n        short_prompt = prompt if len(prompt) < 1000 else prompt[:500] + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n...[TRUNCATED FOR DISPLAY]...\\\\\\\" + prompt[-500:]\\\\n        print(short_prompt)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u23f3 Waiting for agent response...\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        start_time = time.time()\\\\n        try:\\\\n            output = await self._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            \\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 Agent responded in {execution_time:.1f}s\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Output] <- Received from Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            # Hide code blocks for clean UI\\\\n            clean_output = hide_code_blocks(output)\\\\n            print(clean_output)\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            return StageResult(\\\\n                stage_id=stage_def.stage_id,\\\\n                status=\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\",\\\\n                output=output,\\\\n                raw_output=output,\\\\n                execution_time=execution_time,\\\\n                model_used=stage_def.model,\\\\n            )\\\\n            \\\\n        except Exception as exc:\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Stage failed after {execution_time:.1f}s: {exc}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            raise  # Let the base class intercept it and handle fail_fast\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def ensure_servers_running(config_path: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> bool:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Check if required servers are healthy. Return True if all ok.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd0d Checking ACP Server Status...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    report = await check_all_servers(config_path, timeout)\\\\n    if report.unhealthy_count == 0:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 All {report.total_servers} servers are healthy!\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return True\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c SOME SERVERS ARE UNREACHABLE!\\\\\\\")\\\\n    for server in report.servers:\\\\n        if not server.healthy:\\\\n            err = f\\\\\\\" ({server.error})\\\\\\\" if server.error else \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  - {server.base_url} is DOWN{err}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nPlease start the required servers (e.g. using scripts/run_*_server.py).\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return False\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef get_available_workflows() -> list[Path]:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Return a list of workflow YAML files.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    workflows_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / \\\\\\\"workflows\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not workflows_dir.exists():\\\\n        return []\\\\n    return sorted(workflows_dir.glob(\\\\\\\"*.yaml\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def main_async(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\\\n    # 1. Check Servers\\\\n    servers_ok = await ensure_servers_running(args.config, args.timeout)\\\\n    if not servers_ok:\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"               ORCHESTRA MULTI-AGENT CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60 + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # 2. Select Workflow\\\\n    workflows = get_available_workflows()\\\\n    if not workflows:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c No workflows found in the 'workflows/' directory.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    workflow_path = None\\\\n    if args.workflow:\\\\n        workflow_path = Path(args.workflow)\\\\n        if not workflow_path.exists():\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Workflow {workflow_path} not found.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n    else:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"Available Workflows:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        for idx, wf in enumerate(workflows):\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  [{idx + 1}] {wf.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                choice = input(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nSelect a workflow (1-%d): \\\\\\\" % len(workflows))\\\\n                idx = int(choice.strip()) - 1\\\\n                if 0 <= idx < len(workflows):\\\\n                    workflow_path = workflows[idx]\\\\n                    break\\\\n                else:\\\\n                    print(\\\\\\\"Invalid choice.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except ValueError:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"Please enter a number.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n                \\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u2705 Selected workflow: {workflow_path.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # Load pipeline\\\\n    try:\\\\n        pipeline_def = PipelineDefinition(workflow_path)\\\\n    except PipelineError as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Failed to load pipeline: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    # 3. Get Task\\\\n    task_description = args.task\\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nEnter task description (press Enter twice to finish):\\\\\\\")\\\\n        lines = []\\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                line = input()\\\\n                if not line and (not lines or not lines[-1]):\\\\n                    break\\\\n                lines.append(line)\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n        task_description = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(lines).strip()\\\\n        \\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c Task description cannot be empty.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n\\\\n    # 4. Execute Pipeline\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\ud83d\\ude80 Starting Pipeline Execution...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = CLIExecutor(pipeline_def, args.config)\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        # Also map user_task so it's available for workflows explicitly requesting it\\\\n        initial_state = {\\\\\\\"user_task\\\\\\\": task_description}\\\\n        results = await executor.execute(task_description, initial_state=initial_state)\\\\n        \\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83c\\udf89 PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETED\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        for stage_id, result in results.items():\\\\n            icon = \\\\\\\"\\u2705\\\\\\\" if result.status == \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"\\u274c\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"{icon} Stage '{stage_id}': {result.status.upper()} ({result.execution_time:.1f}s)\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        # Check for absolute failure\\\\n        if any(r.status == \\\\\\\"failed\\\\\\\" for r in results.values()):\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline completed with ERRORS.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n            \\\\n    except Exception as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline execution failed due to an error: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        import traceback\\\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n    finally:\\\\n        await executor.cleanup()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef main() -> None:\\\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\\\\\"Interactive Multi-Agent Orchestrator CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--config\\\\\\\", default=\\\\\\\"config/agents.yaml\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to agents.yaml\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--timeout\\\\\\\", type=float, default=3.0, help=\\\\\\\"Per-server health check timeout in seconds\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--workflow\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to a specific workflow YAML, bypasses selection\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--task\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Initial task description, bypasses input dialog\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        asyncio.run(main_async(args))\\\\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExiting CLI...\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(0)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nif __name__ == \\\\\\\"__main__\\\\\\\":\\\\n    main()\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_23\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' harness/db.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_23\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' harness/db.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"SQLite schema and connection helpers for the harness.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport sqlite3\\\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nSCHEMA = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nPRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (\\\\n    task_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    title TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    description TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    goal TEXT,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    complexity TEXT DEFAULT 'medium',\\\\n    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    working_dir TEXT,\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stages (\\\\n    stage_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(task_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    stage_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    stage_order INTEGER NOT NULL,\\\\n    model_role TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    assigned_model TEXT,\\\\n    assigned_provider TEXT,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    handoff_doc_path TEXT,\\\\n    result_summary TEXT,\\\\n    token_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    duration_sec REAL DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    started_at TEXT,\\\\n    finished_at TEXT,\\\\n    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}'\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permission_requests (\\\\n    request_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    stage_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES stages(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    action TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    context TEXT,\\\\n    risk_level TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    decision TEXT,\\\\n    voters TEXT DEFAULT '[]',\\\\n    decided_at TEXT\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quota_events (\\\\n    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    provider TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    account_email TEXT,\\\\n    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status_priority\\\\n    ON tasks(status, priority DESC, created_at ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_order\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_status\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, status, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass HarnessDB:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Small wrapper around a SQLite database file.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:\\\\n        self.db_path = Path(db_path)\\\\n        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n        self.initialize()\\\\n\\\\n    def initialize(self) -> None:\\\\n        with self.connect() as conn:\\\\n -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,240p' artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Output: plan\\\\n## Summary\\\\nImplement the robustness work in three slices, keeping `QuotaRouter` selection pure and pushing all operational side effects into the harness runtime / CLI layer.\\\\n\\\\n1. Codex account rotation\\\\n- Keep `QuotaRouter.select_model()` and `can_run_stage()` side-effect free.\\\\n- Change Codex candidate selection from \\\\\\\"best provider snapshot\\\\\\\" to \\\\\\\"best eligible Codex account snapshot\\\\\\\", using the existing use-it-or-lose-it score.\\\\n- Preserve the chosen `account_email` on the selected stage, preferably in stage metadata if the DB schema should stay stable.\\\\n- Add an explicit pre-execution Codex activation step that calls `models.codex.account.get_active_email()` and `set_active_account()` only when the chosen account differs.\\\\n\\\\n2. Daemon lifecycle\\\\n- Use the harness CLI in [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) as the management entrypoint, not the older workflow CLI in [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py).\\\\n- Add `daemon start`, `daemon stop`, `daemon status`, plus a hidden worker subcommand that runs `Harness.run_forever()`.\\\\n- Prefer a portable detached `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` / nohup-style path for tests instead of systemd-specific logic.\\\\n- Persist PID metadata, detect stale PID files, and handle `SIGTERM` so the loop exits cleanly after the current iteration.\\\\n\\\\n3. Logging, monitoring, and dashboard\\\\n- Add centralized logging setup for harness CLI + daemon startup.\\\\n- Emit structured JSONL stage events for start, success, retry, quota pause, permission pause, and terminal failure.\\\\n- Reuse persisted `stages.token_used` and `stages.duration_sec` for aggregates; parse provider output for token usage where available and leave `0` when unavailable.\\\\n- Add a `dashboard` command that combines DB state, quota/permission audit tables, daemon state, and recent JSONL events into one status summary.\\\\n\\\\n## Key File Targets\\\\n- [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py)\\\\n- [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py)\\\\n- [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py)\\\\n- [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py)\\\\n- [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py)\\\\n- [config/settings.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/config/settings.py)\\\\n- New modules are justified for daemon management and telemetry aggregation.\\\\n\\\\n## Risks To Avoid\\\\n- Do not let quota feasibility checks mutate global Codex auth state.\\\\n- Do not add daemon logic to the legacy orchestrator CLI; keep it on the harness path.\\\\n- Do not rely on live systemd in tests; use deterministic unit seams around PID files, signals, and subprocess launch.\\\\n\\\\n## Test Plan\\\\n- Extend router tests for multi-account Codex ranking and no-side-effect feasibility checks.\\\\n- Add harness tests for Codex account activation before a Codex stage executes.\\\\n- Add daemon tests for PID lifecycle, stale PID cleanup, and graceful stop/status behavior.\\\\n- Add telemetry/dashboard tests for JSONL emission and aggregate summaries.\\\\n- Final verification: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\\\\n\\\\n## Baseline\\\\nCurrent baseline is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` passed with `21 passed`.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_33\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/02_code_handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_33\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/02_code_handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Handoff: plan -> code\\\\n## Task\\\\nPhase 4: \\u5065\\u58ee\\u6027 for code-orchestra harness\\\\n\\\\n## Context Gathered\\\\n- `QuotaRouter` currently collapses each provider to a single best snapshot in [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py), which is not enough for Codex multi-account rotation.\\\\n- `Harness._run_stage()` in [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) persists model/provider selection but has no Codex account activation, no daemon hooks, and no telemetry hooks.\\\\n- `models.codex.account.set_active_account()` already handles token refresh + auth file switching in [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py); the harness should integrate it as a runtime step, not from the router.\\\\n- `StageExecutionResult` already has `token_used` and `duration_sec`, and the stages table already persists both via [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py).\\\\n- Baseline verification is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` => `21 passed`.\\\\n\\\\n## Recommended Implementation Order\\\\n1. Codex rotation\\\\n- Update router eligibility/fallback logic so Codex models consider all eligible Codex snapshots, keeping the chosen `account_email`.\\\\n- Persist the selected account on the stage.\\\\n- Add a small helper in the harness path to activate the selected Codex account just before execution.\\\\n- Add tests first for router ranking, then for harness-side activation.\\\\n\\\\n2. Daemon mode\\\\n- Add a dedicated daemon helper module with PID file read/write, process liveness checks, start/stop/status, and stale PID cleanup.\\\\n- Extend the harness parser with `daemon` and a hidden worker command.\\\\n- Add `SIGTERM`-aware stop handling around `run_forever`, ideally with an `asyncio.Event` / shutdown flag seam that is easy to unit test.\\\\n\\\\n3. Telemetry + dashboard\\\\n- Add centralized logging config and a JSONL event writer module.\\\\n- Emit stage lifecycle events from the harness runtime, not from the router.\\\\n- Add a dashboard summary builder that reads DB state + event log + daemon status.\\\\n- Expose it via `dashboard` in the harness CLI.\\\\n\\\\n## Concrete Guidance\\\\n- Prefer stage metadata for the selected Codex account instead of widening the DB schema unless a wider schema clearly improves readability.\\\\n- Keep `can_run_stage()` pure; it is called from paused-task recovery and should not switch accounts.\\\\n- Parse token usage opportunistically in [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py); do not block this phase on provider-specific token support.\\\\n- Treat [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py) as unrelated legacy workflow UI unless you discover a hard requirement otherwise.\\\\n\\\\n## Verification Target\\\\nRun `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` after each slice if possible, then once at the end.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.updated\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_27\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"todo_list\\\",\\\"items\\\":[{\\\"text\\\":\\\"Inspect artifacts/83b2ef3d77cf4f508fdea0bd372794e7/02_code_handoff.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Handoff: plan -> code\n## Task\nPhase 4: 健壮性 for code-orchestra harness\n\n## Context Gathered\n- [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py) 当前按 provider 取单个最佳 quota snapshot；这会丢掉 Codex 的账号级选择空间。\n- [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) 当前只持久化 `assigned_model` / `assigned_provider`，执行前没有 Codex 账号激活，也没有 daemon / telemetry hook。\n- [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py) 已经有 `metadata` 字段，但缺少单独的 metadata 更新接口；这是保存 `account_email` 的最小改动点。\n- [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py) 已经返回 `duration_sec` / `token_used`，其中 `token_used` 目前始终接近未使用状态，适合在这里补 provider 输出解析。\n- [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py) 已提供 `get_active_email()` 和 `set_active_account()`；账号切换副作用应放到 runtime，而不是 router。\n- 当前基线: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` 通过，结果是 `21 passed`。\n\n## Recommended Order\n1. 先做 Codex 轮换\n- router 改成账号级候选。\n- stage metadata 持久化 `selected_account_email`。\n- `Harness._run_stage()` 在真正执行前切换 Codex 账号。\n- 先补 router 和 harness 测试，再动实现。\n\n2. 再做 daemon\n- 新建 daemon helper 模块，不要把 PID/信号逻辑塞满 `main.py`。\n- parser 增加 `daemon` 子命令和内部 worker 子命令。\n- `run_forever()` 增加可优雅退出的 stop seam。\n\n3. 最后做 telemetry 和 dashboard\n- 统一 logging 配置。\n- stage 生命周期事件写 JSONL。\n- `dashboard` 聚合 DB + daemon + recent events。\n\n## Concrete Guidance\n- `QuotaRouter.can_run_stage()` 必须保持无副作用，因为它会在 paused quota 恢复逻辑中反复调用。\n- `TaskQueue.assign_stage_model()` 很可能需要扩成同时支持 metadata patch，或者新增 `update_stage_metadata()`。\n- daemon 测试应 mock `Popen`、`os.kill(pid, 0)` 或等价存活检查，避免真实后台进程依赖。\n- `dashboard` 不必做复杂 TUI，先输出稳定 JSON/结构化文本即可。\n\n## Verification Target\n收尾时运行: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Codex 多账号轮换\n- 修改 [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py)，把 `codex` 候选从“按 provider 取一个最佳 snapshot”改成“按账号展开后逐个打分”，继续复用 `QuotaManager._score_account()` 的 use-it-or-lose-it 逻辑。\n- 保持 `QuotaRouter.select_model()` / `can_run_stage()` 纯函数化，不在路由阶段切账号。\n- 在 stage 上持久化被选中的 `account_email`。优先放到 `metadata`，并补一个 `TaskQueue` 的 metadata 更新接口，避免为此扩表。\n- 在 [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) 的执行路径里增加 Codex 账号激活步骤，仅当 `assigned_provider == \"codex\"` 且目标账号与 `models.codex.account.get_active_email()` 不同时调用 `set_active_account()`。\n\n2. daemon 后台模式\n- 以 [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) 作为管理入口，新增 `daemon start|stop|status` 和一个内部 worker 子命令。\n- 新增独立 daemon helper 模块，负责 PID 文件、metadata 文件、存活检测、stale PID 清理和后台拉起。\n- 后台运行优先走可测试的 `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` / nohup 风格实现，不把 systemd 作为单元测试依赖。\n- 给 `run_forever()` 增加 `SIGTERM` 可控退出路径，最好通过 shutdown flag / `asyncio.Event` 做出可测试 seam。\n\n3. logging / telemetry / dashboard\n- 新增统一 logging 配置，CLI 和 daemon 启动时共用。\n- 在 harness runtime 发出结构化 JSONL stage 事件: `stage_started`、`stage_succeeded`、`stage_retry`、`stage_paused_quota`、`stage_paused_permission`、`stage_failed`、`task_finished`。\n- `token_used` 和 `duration_sec` 继续复用现有表字段；在 [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py) 里对 Codex JSON 输出做轻量 token 解析，拿不到时保持 `0`。\n- 新增 `dashboard` 命令，聚合 tasks/stages、quota_events、permission_requests、daemon 状态和最近 JSONL 事件，输出当前概览。\n\n## Key Constraints\n- 不要让 quota 检查触发全局 Codex auth 切换。\n- 不要把 daemon 管理接到旧的 [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py)。\n- 不要依赖真实 systemd 做测试。\n\n## Test Additions\n- 扩展 [tests/harness/test_quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_quota_router.py): 多 Codex 账号排序、`can_run_stage()` 无副作用。\n- 扩展 [tests/harness/test_main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_main.py): stage 选择账号后执行前激活。\n- 新增 daemon 测试: PID 生命周期、stale PID、graceful stop/status。\n- 新增 telemetry/dashboard 测试: JSONL 事件落盘与汇总统计。\n\n## Verification\n最终验收命令: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
